data.purpose: Handles chat completion requests in Anthropic Claude format, supporting both streaming and non-streaming responses with model routing, caching, failover, token budget enforcement, and usage tracking.

and: Request body is parsed and validated
Model is checked against allowlist
Token budget is verified
Cache is checked for exact hits
Connection is selected from pool with failover support
Streaming responses use SSE format
Non-streaming responses return complete JSON
Usage is accumulated for billing
Request/response may be compressed
scenarios.given: Authenticated user with valid API key and allowed model access
scenarios.name: Streaming chat completion
scenarios.then: Returns SSE stream with Claude-format message deltas or full message response
scenarios.when: POST /v1/messages with Anthropic-compatible request body including stream:true

and: Request body is parsed and validated
Model is checked against allowlist
Token budget is verified
Cache is checked for exact hits
Connection is selected from pool with failover support
Full response is collected before returning
Usage is accumulated for billing
scenarios.given: Authenticated user with valid API key and allowed model access
scenarios.name: Non-streaming chat completion
scenarios.then: Returns 200 OK with complete Claude-format message object
scenarios.when: POST /v1/messages with Anthropic-compatible request body including stream:false or omitted

and: Request body exceeds size limit
Or request contains invalid JSON
Or model is not in allowed list
Or token budget is exceeded
scenarios.given: Any client making a request
scenarios.name: Request validation failure
scenarios.then: Returns 400 Bad Request with claudeError JSON detailing the failure
scenarios.when: POST /v1/messages with invalid or disallowed parameters

and: All connections in pool are in cooldown
Or all connections fail health checks
Or provider returns persistent errors
scenarios.given: Authenticated user with valid request
scenarios.name: Failover exhaustion
scenarios.then: Returns 503 Service Unavailable or builds failover error response with retry guidance
scenarios.when: POST /v1/messages when no healthy connections available after failover attempts

data.purpose: Counts tokens for a given Anthropic-format message request using the provider's native token counting endpoint, returning token usage without generating a completion.

and: Request body is parsed and validated
Model is checked against allowlist
Executor is resolved for the model
Connection is obtained from pool
Provider's CountTokens is called
Result is returned with proper headers
scenarios.given: Authenticated user with valid API key and allowed model access
scenarios.name: Successful token count
scenarios.then: Returns 200 OK with token count in Anthropic-compatible format
scenarios.when: POST /v1/messages/count_tokens with valid message request body

and: Request body is invalid
Or model is not allowed
Or executor resolution fails
Or no connection available
scenarios.given: Any client making a request
scenarios.name: Token count failure
scenarios.then: Returns 400 Bad Request with claudeError JSON
scenarios.when: POST /v1/messages/count_tokens with invalid parameters

data.purpose: Generate vector embeddings for input text using a specified AI model. Routes to appropriate provider (OpenAI-compatible), validates model permissions, checks token budgets, and returns embedding vectors.

and: Response includes embedding vectors in OpenAI-compatible format
Usage metrics are accumulated for the API key
scenarios.given: Authenticated user with valid API key and allowed model access
scenarios.name: Successful embedding generation
scenarios.then: 200 OK with embedding vectors array
scenarios.when: POST /v1/embeddings with valid model and input text

and: Error type is 'invalid_request_error'
scenarios.given: Any request to embeddings endpoint
scenarios.name: Missing model field
scenarios.then: 400 Bad Request with 'model is required' error
scenarios.when: POST /v1/embeddings without 'model' in request body

and: Error type is 'invalid_request_error'
scenarios.given: Authenticated user with restricted model access
scenarios.name: Model not allowed
scenarios.then: 403 Forbidden with 'model not allowed for this API key' error
scenarios.when: POST /v1/embeddings with model not in allowed list

and: Token budget check fails
scenarios.given: Authenticated user with exceeded or invalid token budget
scenarios.name: Token budget exceeded
scenarios.then: 429 or appropriate error status with budget exceeded message
scenarios.when: POST /v1/embeddings when token budget is depleted

and: Error type is 'invalid_request_error'
scenarios.given: Provider configuration exists but lacks embedding support
scenarios.name: Provider missing embedding service kind
scenarios.then: 400 Bad Request with 'provider does not support embeddings' error
scenarios.when: POST /v1/embeddings with provider not configured for embeddings

and: Error type is 'invalid_request_error'
scenarios.given: Provider has modality restrictions configured
scenarios.name: Model not available for embeddings on provider
scenarios.then: 400 Bad Request with model availability error for specific provider
scenarios.when: POST /v1/embeddings with model not in provider's embedding modality list

and: Error type is 'server_error'
scenarios.given: No healthy connections available for provider
scenarios.name: No available connection
scenarios.then: 503 Service Unavailable with 'no available connection' error
scenarios.when: POST /v1/embeddings when all provider connections are down

and: Error type is 'invalid_request_error'
scenarios.given: Resolved executor does not implement EmbeddingsExecutor interface
scenarios.name: Non-OpenAI compatible provider
scenarios.then: 400 Bad Request with 'embeddings only supported for OpenAI-compatible providers' error
scenarios.when: POST /v1/embeddings with provider that doesn't support OpenAI-compatible embeddings

data.purpose: Generate images from text prompts using AI model providers. Handles request validation, model permission checks, token budget enforcement, provider resolution, and streaming/non-streaming execution through the executor framework.

and: Request body is parsed and validated
Model is checked against allowed models list
Token budget is validated for the requesting organization
Provider alias is resolved to concrete service
Executor is created with appropriate configuration
Request is executed with retry logic
API usage is accumulated for billing
Response is proxied back to client with appropriate headers
scenarios.given: Authenticated user with valid API key and sufficient token budget
scenarios.name: Successful image generation
scenarios.then: 200 OK with generated image data in response body
scenarios.when: POST /v1/images/generations with valid prompt and model

and: Error response is written with appropriate status code
Usage may still be accumulated if applicable
scenarios.given: Request with invalid JSON body or missing required fields
scenarios.name: Invalid request body
scenarios.then: 400 Bad Request with error details
scenarios.when: POST /v1/images/generations with malformed JSON

and: Request is aborted before reaching executor
Error is logged for monitoring
scenarios.given: Authenticated user requesting disallowed model
scenarios.name: Model not allowed
scenarios.then: 403 Forbidden or 400 Bad Request with model access error
scenarios.when: POST /v1/images/generations with unauthorized model ID

and: Request is aborted with JSON error response
No executor is created or invoked
scenarios.given: Organization with exhausted or insufficient token budget
scenarios.name: Token budget exceeded
scenarios.then: 429 Too Many Requests or 402 Payment Required with budget error
scenarios.when: POST /v1/images/generations when token budget is depleted

and: Retry logic may be attempted depending on error type
Upstream error is proxied to client with appropriate wrapping
scenarios.given: Valid request but upstream provider failure
scenarios.name: Upstream provider error
scenarios.then: 502 Bad Gateway or 503 Service Unavailable with provider error details
scenarios.when: POST /v1/images/generations when provider service fails

data.purpose: Handles speech-to-text transcription requests by receiving multipart audio uploads, validating model permissions, checking token budgets, routing to appropriate STT executor with connection pooling and retry logic, and accumulating API usage metrics.

and: Request Content-Type header is parsed for multipart boundary
Audio file is extracted from multipart form data
Model ID is validated against allowed models from context
Token budget is checked for the requesting organization
Connection is selected from pool with health/cooldown checks
Request is forwarded to upstream STT provider with retry logic
API usage is accumulated for billing/metrics
Response is streamed back to client with appropriate headers
scenarios.given: Authenticated user with valid JWT, allowed model access, and sufficient token budget
scenarios.name: Successful STT transcription
scenarios.then: 200 OK with transcription result from upstream provider
scenarios.when: POST /v1/audio/transcriptions with multipart audio file and model parameter

and: Model ID is checked against allowed models list from context
Request is rejected before processing audio data
scenarios.given: Authenticated user with valid JWT but requesting disallowed model
scenarios.name: Model not allowed
scenarios.then: Error response indicating model is not permitted
scenarios.when: POST /v1/audio/transcriptions with unauthorized model ID

and: Organization ID extracted from context
Token budget retrieved and compared against usage
Request is rejected if budget exceeded
scenarios.given: Authenticated user whose organization has exceeded token budget
scenarios.name: Token budget exceeded
scenarios.then: Error response indicating insufficient token budget
scenarios.when: POST /v1/audio/transcriptions when organization quota is depleted

and: Connection pool is exhausted or all connections in cooldown
Fallback logic attempts older connection selection
Request fails if no viable connection found
scenarios.given: All STT provider connections are unhealthy or in cooldown
scenarios.name: No available connections
scenarios.then: Error response indicating no available upstream connection
scenarios.when: POST /v1/audio/transcriptions when connection pool has no healthy endpoints

and: Multipart body is read and preserved for forwarding
Content-Type with boundary is maintained for upstream request
File handles are properly closed after reading
scenarios.given: Valid multipart request with audio file attachment
scenarios.name: Multipart body forwarding
scenarios.then: Audio data successfully forwarded to upstream STT provider
scenarios.when: POST /v1/audio/transcriptions with multipart/form-data content

data.purpose: Convert text to speech using a specified model and voice, returning synthesized audio content. Validates model permissions, checks token budgets, manages provider connection selection with fallback, and tracks API usage.

and: Response includes Content-Type header matching requested audio format
X-Request-ID header is set with unique trace identifier
API usage is accumulated for billing
scenarios.given: Authenticated user with valid JWT, allowed model access, and sufficient token budget
scenarios.name: Successful TTS generation
scenarios.then: 200 OK with audio/mpeg or requested audio format stream
scenarios.when: POST /v1/audio/speech with valid TTS request body containing model, input text, and voice

and: Error response follows standard error format with code and message
scenarios.given: Authenticated user with disallowed model in request
scenarios.name: Model not allowed
scenarios.then: 400 Bad Request with model access denied error
scenarios.when: POST /v1/audio/speech with model not in user's allowed models list

and: Request is aborted before upstream call
scenarios.given: Authenticated user with insufficient token budget for requested input
scenarios.name: Token budget exceeded
scenarios.then: 429 Too Many Requests or 400 Bad Request with budget exceeded error
scenarios.when: POST /v1/audio/speech with input exceeding configured token budget

and: Error is logged for observability
Client receives sanitized error without upstream details
scenarios.given: Valid request but all provider connections fail
scenarios.name: Provider connection failure
scenarios.then: 502 Bad Gateway or 503 Service Unavailable with upstream connection error
scenarios.when: POST /v1/audio/speech and getConnection exhausts all provider fallbacks

and: Request body size limit is enforced via http.MaxBytesReader
scenarios.given: Request with body exceeding maximum allowed size
scenarios.name: Request body too large
scenarios.then: 413 Payload Too Large or 400 Bad Request with body read error
scenarios.when: POST /v1/audio/speech with request body larger than configured max bytes

data.purpose: Unified endpoint that dispatches to the appropriate handler based on request mode. Supports chat completions, images, and video generation modes by inspecting the request body and routing to the corresponding internal handler.

and: Request body contains valid mode field
Model is allowed for the authenticated user
Token budget is sufficient
scenarios.given: Authenticated user with valid JWT token and request within size limits
scenarios.name: Chat completions mode
scenarios.then: Request is dispatched to ChatCompletions handler with streaming or non-streaming response based on stream flag
scenarios.when: POST /v1/unified with mode=chat or inferred chat completion request

and: Request body contains image generation parameters
Model is allowed for the authenticated user
scenarios.given: Authenticated user with valid JWT token
scenarios.name: Images generation mode
scenarios.then: Request is dispatched to Images handler for image generation
scenarios.when: POST /v1/unified with mode=images or inferred image generation request

and: Request body contains video generation parameters
Model is allowed for the authenticated user
scenarios.given: Authenticated user with valid JWT token
scenarios.name: Video generation mode
scenarios.then: Request is dispatched to Video handler for video generation
scenarios.when: POST /v1/unified with mode=video or inferred video generation request

and: Request body exceeds maximum size limit
scenarios.given: Any request with body larger than configured max bytes
scenarios.name: Request body too large
scenarios.then: 413 Payload Too Large with read body error details
scenarios.when: POST /v1/unified with oversized request body

and: User has exceeded token budget or model is not allowed
scenarios.given: Authenticated user with exhausted token budget or unauthorized model
scenarios.name: Budget or permission denied
scenarios.then: 403 Forbidden or appropriate error response with budget/permission details
scenarios.when: POST /v1/unified with disallowed model or exceeded budget

data.purpose: Handle chat completion requests including streaming and non-streaming responses. Supports model fusion, combo requests, caching, failover between providers, token usage tracking, and rate limit handling.

and: Model is allowed via modelIDAllowed check
Token budget sufficient via checkTokenBudget
Optional fusion/combo detection for multi-model requests
Cache lookup for exact match
Provider resolution and proxy candidate selection
scenarios.given: Authenticated user with valid request
scenarios.name: Non-streaming chat completion
scenarios.then: 200 OK with JSON completion response including usage statistics, cached or from upstream provider
scenarios.when: POST /v1/unified dispatched to ChatCompletions with stream=false or no stream flag

and: Streaming enabled via stream=true or stream options
Model supports streaming
Provider connection established
scenarios.given: Authenticated user requesting streaming response
scenarios.name: Streaming chat completion
scenarios.then: 200 OK with text/event-stream or NDJSON chunked response, with proper cancellation handling and failover on errors
scenarios.when: POST /v1/unified dispatched to ChatCompletions with stream=true

and: Fusion mode detected via combo.DetectRequiredCapabilities
Multiple models required for request
scenarios.given: Authenticated user with complex multi-model requirement
scenarios.name: Fusion request handling
scenarios.then: Request processed through handleFusionRequest combining multiple model capabilities
scenarios.when: POST /v1/unified with fusion-capable model configuration

and: Exact cache key match found
Cache entry is fresh
scenarios.given: Authenticated user with previously cached identical request
scenarios.name: Cache hit serving
scenarios.then: 200 OK with cached response body, skipping upstream provider call
scenarios.when: POST /v1/unified with exactCacheKey matching stored entry

and: Primary provider fails with eligible error
Failover candidates available
Ban count allows retry
scenarios.given: Authenticated user with failing primary provider
scenarios.name: Provider failover
scenarios.then: Request retried with next available provider, exponential backoff applied, ban count tracked
scenarios.when: POST /v1/unified where primary provider returns failover-eligible error

data.purpose: Handle image generation requests by dispatching to the Images handler. Converts request to appropriate format for image generation providers.

and: Model is allowed for image generation
Request contains valid image generation parameters
scenarios.given: Authenticated user with image generation request
scenarios.name: Image generation
scenarios.then: 200 OK with generated image data or URL in response format matching OpenAI images API
scenarios.when: POST /v1/unified dispatched to Images handler

data.purpose: Handle video generation requests by dispatching to the Video handler. Converts request to appropriate format for video generation providers.

and: Model is allowed for video generation
Request contains valid video generation parameters
scenarios.given: Authenticated user with video generation request
scenarios.name: Video generation
scenarios.then: 200 OK with video generation task status or result
scenarios.when: POST /v1/unified dispatched to Video handler

data.purpose: Generate a video from a text prompt or image input using AI models. Handles model validation, token budget checking, connection pooling to upstream providers, request proxying with retry logic, and usage accumulation.

and: Upstream provider returns 200 with video generation result
API key usage is accumulated for billing
scenarios.given: Authenticated user with valid JWT, allowed model ID, and sufficient token budget
scenarios.name: Successful video generation
scenarios.then: 200 OK with generated video content proxied from upstream provider
scenarios.when: POST /v1/video/generations is called with valid request body containing model and prompt

and: Error response indicates model not allowed
scenarios.given: Authenticated user with disallowed model ID in request
scenarios.name: Model not allowed
scenarios.then: 400 Bad Request with error indicating model is not allowed
scenarios.when: POST /v1/video/generations is called with unauthorized model ID

and: Error response indicates insufficient budget
scenarios.given: Authenticated user with exceeded token budget
scenarios.name: Token budget exceeded
scenarios.then: 429 Too Many Requests or 403 Forbidden with budget exceeded error
scenarios.when: POST /v1/video/generations is called when token budget is depleted

and: Retry logic may attempt fallback connections
Error is logged for observability
scenarios.given: Valid request but all upstream connections fail
scenarios.name: Upstream connection failure
scenarios.then: 502 Bad Gateway or 503 Service Unavailable with upstream error details
scenarios.when: POST /v1/video/generations is called and no healthy upstream provider is available

and: Request body size is limited by http.MaxBytesReader
scenarios.given: Request with excessively large body
scenarios.name: Request body too large
scenarios.then: 413 Payload Too Large with body read error
scenarios.when: POST /v1/video/generations is called with request body exceeding size limit

data.purpose: Handle OpenAI Responses API format requests with support for streaming, model routing, caching, failover, compression, token budgeting, and quota management

and: Request body is read and optionally compressed
Model is validated against allowlist
Token budget is checked
Exact cache is checked for hits
Capabilities are detected for routing
scenarios.given: Authenticated user with valid API key and request body in OpenAI Responses format
scenarios.name: Non-streaming successful response
scenarios.then: 200 OK with JSON response body containing output, usage, and id
scenarios.when: POST /v1/responses with stream: false

and: Request body is read and optionally compressed
Model is validated against allowlist
Token budget is checked
Exact cache is checked for hits
Streaming executor is resolved and executed
scenarios.given: Authenticated user with valid API key and request body in OpenAI Responses format
scenarios.name: Streaming successful response
scenarios.then: 200 OK with chunked SSE stream containing response deltas
scenarios.when: POST /v1/responses with stream: true

and: Request body is read and optionally compressed
Model is validated against allowlist
Token budget is checked
scenarios.given: Authenticated user with valid API key
scenarios.name: Token budget exceeded
scenarios.then: 429 Too Many Requests or 400 Bad Request with error about token budget
scenarios.when: POST /v1/responses with request exceeding configured token budget

and: Request body is read and optionally compressed
Model validation fails
scenarios.given: Authenticated user with valid API key
scenarios.name: Model not allowed
scenarios.then: 400 Bad Request with error about disallowed model
scenarios.when: POST /v1/responses with model not in allowlist

and: Request body read fails due to size limit
scenarios.given: Any client request
scenarios.name: Request body too large
scenarios.then: 413 Payload Too Large with error message
scenarios.when: POST /v1/responses with body exceeding MaxBytesReader limit

and: Exact cache key matches previous request
scenarios.given: Authenticated user with identical previous request
scenarios.name: Exact cache hit
scenarios.then: 200 OK with cached response body, skipping upstream call
scenarios.when: POST /v1/responses with matching cache key

and: Primary upstream fails with eligible error
Failover is configured
scenarios.given: Authenticated user with failing primary upstream
scenarios.name: Failover to secondary upstream
scenarios.then: 200 OK with response from failover upstream, or aggregated error if all fail
scenarios.when: POST /v1/responses during upstream outage

and: Quota exhaustion detected for model scope
scenarios.given: Authenticated user with exhausted quota
scenarios.name: Quota cooldown applied
scenarios.then: Response with cooldown headers, quota exhaustion persisted to cache
scenarios.when: POST /v1/responses for model in cooldown period

data.purpose: Deploy a relay edge function to Vercel's edge network, creating a new proxy pool entry with generated authentication and testing the deployed endpoint before saving.

and: Polls Vercel deployment status until ready
Tests the deployed relay URL for health
Saves the new proxy pool to database with active status
scenarios.given: Authenticated admin with valid Vercel deployment credentials and relay configuration
scenarios.name: Successful Vercel deployment
scenarios.then: Returns 200 OK with deployment details and saved pool information
scenarios.when: POST /api/proxy-pools/vercel-deploy is called with deployment parameters

and: Returns parsed Vercel error message from response body
scenarios.given: Vercel API returns non-2xx status during deployment creation
scenarios.name: Vercel API error
scenarios.then: Returns 500 Internal Server Error with descriptive error message
scenarios.when: POST /api/proxy-pools/vercel-deploy is called

and: Deployment polling exceeds configured timeout duration
scenarios.given: Vercel deployment creation succeeds but deployment never reaches ready state
scenarios.name: Vercel deployment timeout
scenarios.then: Returns 500 Internal Server Error with timeout error message
scenarios.when: POST /api/proxy-pools/vercel-deploy is called

and: Relay health check fails after successful deployment
scenarios.given: Vercel deployment succeeds but deployed relay endpoint fails health check
scenarios.name: Relay health check failure
scenarios.then: Returns 500 Internal Server Error with relay test failure details
scenarios.when: POST /api/proxy-pools/vercel-deploy is called

data.purpose: Deploy a relay edge function to Deno Deploy's edge network, creating a new proxy pool entry with generated authentication and testing the deployed endpoint before saving.

and: Polls Deno deployment revision status until ready
Tests the deployed relay URL for health
Saves the new proxy pool to database with active status
scenarios.given: Authenticated admin with valid Deno Deploy token and relay configuration
scenarios.name: Successful Deno deployment
scenarios.then: Returns 200 OK with deployment details and saved pool information
scenarios.when: POST /api/proxy-pools/deno-deploy is called with deployment parameters

and: Returns parsed Deno error message from response body
scenarios.given: Deno Deploy API returns non-2xx status during deployment
scenarios.name: Deno API error
scenarios.then: Returns 500 Internal Server Error with descriptive error message
scenarios.when: POST /api/proxy-pools/deno-deploy is called

and: Revision polling exceeds configured timeout duration
scenarios.given: Deno deployment succeeds but revision never reaches ready state
scenarios.name: Deno revision timeout
scenarios.then: Returns 500 Internal Server Error with timeout error message
scenarios.when: POST /api/proxy-pools/deno-deploy is called

and: Organization slug is normalized from project identifier
scenarios.given: Deno project identifier includes organization prefix or trailing slash
scenarios.name: Organization slug normalization
scenarios.then: Correctly extracts and formats organization slug for API calls
scenarios.when: POST /api/proxy-pools/deno-deploy is called with org-prefixed project

data.purpose: Deploy a relay edge function to Cloudflare Workers, creating a new proxy pool entry with generated authentication and testing the deployed endpoint before saving.

and: Uploads worker script to Cloudflare
Deploys worker to edge
Tests the deployed relay URL for health
Saves the new proxy pool to database with active status
scenarios.given: Authenticated admin with valid Cloudflare API credentials and relay configuration
scenarios.name: Successful Cloudflare deployment
scenarios.then: Returns 200 OK with deployment details and saved pool information
scenarios.when: POST /api/proxy-pools/cloudflare-deploy is called with deployment parameters

and: Returns parsed Cloudflare error message from response body
scenarios.given: Cloudflare API returns non-2xx status during script upload or deployment
scenarios.name: Cloudflare API error
scenarios.then: Returns 500 Internal Server Error with descriptive error message
scenarios.when: POST /api/proxy-pools/cloudflare-deploy is called

and: Deployment status polling indicates failure
scenarios.given: Cloudflare script upload succeeds but deployment fails
scenarios.name: Cloudflare deployment failure
scenarios.then: Returns 500 Internal Server Error with deployment failure details
scenarios.when: POST /api/proxy-pools/cloudflare-deploy is called

data.purpose: Generate the edge function source code for a specified relay type without deploying, returning the complete runnable edge function code that can be manually deployed.

and: Replaces template variables with generated values
Returns complete edge function source as string
scenarios.given: Authenticated admin with valid relay type specification
scenarios.name: Generate edge function source
scenarios.then: Returns 200 OK with generated source code in response body
scenarios.when: POST /api/proxy-pools/generate-source is called with relay type parameter

and: Relay type must be one of supported edge platforms

scenarios.name: Invalid relay type
scenarios.then: Returns 400 Bad Request with validation error
scenarios.when: POST /api/proxy-pools/generate-source is called with unknown relay type

data.purpose: List all proxy groups with optional filtering and pagination.

and: Supports pagination via query parameters
Supports filtering by active status

scenarios.name: List proxy groups
scenarios.then: Returns a paginated list of proxy groups
scenarios.when: GET /api/admin/proxy-groups is called with optional query parameters

data.purpose: Get a single proxy group by its ID.

and: Returns 404 if proxy group does not exist

scenarios.name: Get proxy group
scenarios.then: Returns the proxy group details

data.purpose: Create a new proxy group with associated proxy pools.

and: Validates required fields
Processes pool IDs from request
Generates UUID for new group

scenarios.name: Create proxy group
scenarios.then: Creates and returns the new proxy group

data.purpose: Update an existing proxy group's configuration and associated pools.

and: Validates proxy group exists before update
Processes pool IDs from request
Updates timestamp on modification

scenarios.then: Updates and returns the modified proxy group

scenarios.name: Delete proxy group
scenarios.then: Deletes the proxy group and returns success

data.purpose: Returns the current master key, its prefix, the admin base URL, and when the key was created.

and: Response body contains key, prefix, base_url, and created_at fields

scenarios.then: 200 OK with master key metadata in response body

data.purpose: Rotates the master key and returns the new metadata.

and: New master key is persisted
Response contains new key, prefix, base_url, and created_at

data.purpose: Returns current compression configuration including mode and lite settings

and: Response includes mode and lite configuration object
scenarios.given: Admin user is authenticated
scenarios.name: Retrieve compression settings
scenarios.then: 200 OK with compression configuration JSON
scenarios.when: GET /admin/optimization/compression is called

Returns updated compression settings

scenarios.then: 200 OK with updated compression configuration

and: Error details returned in response

scenarios.then: 400 Bad Request with error message
scenarios.when: PUT /admin/optimization/compression is called with malformed request

data.purpose: Returns current cache statistics including hits, misses, size and hit rate

and: Response includes hits, misses, size and calculated hit_rate
scenarios.given: Admin user is authenticated
scenarios.name: Retrieve cache statistics
scenarios.then: 200 OK with cache statistics JSON
scenarios.when: GET /admin/optimization/cache/stats is called

data.purpose: Clears all cached responses from the cache storage

and: All cache entries are removed
Confirmation flag returned
scenarios.given: Admin user is authenticated
scenarios.name: Flush cache
scenarios.then: 200 OK with flushed confirmation
scenarios.when: POST /admin/optimization/cache/flush is called

data.purpose: Returns aggregate compression statistics per mode and totals from database

and: Response includes per-mode breakdown with tokens_saved and savings_percent
Totals calculated across all modes
scenarios.given: Admin user is authenticated and metrics exist in database
scenarios.name: Retrieve compression metrics
scenarios.then: 200 OK with compression metrics JSON
scenarios.when: GET /admin/optimization/compression/metrics is called

and: Error details returned in response
scenarios.given: Database query fails
scenarios.name: Database error retrieving metrics
scenarios.then: 500 Internal Server Error with error message
scenarios.when: GET /admin/optimization/compression/metrics encounters database error

data.purpose: Runs compression on a sample body and returns compression statistics without persisting

and: Uses current DB lite configuration when mode is lite
Returns compressed text and token statistics
scenarios.given: Admin user provides sample body text
scenarios.name: Preview compression on sample
scenarios.then: 200 OK with compression preview results
scenarios.when: POST /admin/optimization/compression/preview is called with body and optional mode

and: Error details returned in response
scenarios.given: Admin user omits required body field
scenarios.name: Missing body in preview request
scenarios.then: 400 Bad Request with error message
scenarios.when: POST /admin/optimization/compression/preview is called without body

data.purpose: List quotas with filtering by provider, search term, and status, with pagination support.

and: Response includes paginated list of quotas
scenarios.given: Admin user is authenticated
scenarios.name: List quotas with default pagination
scenarios.then: 200 OK with list of quotas
scenarios.when: GET /api/admin/quota is called with no query parameters

and: Only quotas matching filters are returned
Pagination metadata is included
scenarios.given: Admin user is authenticated and quotas exist for provider 'aws'
scenarios.name: List quotas with provider filter
scenarios.then: 200 OK with filtered quota list
scenarios.when: GET /api/admin/quota?provider=aws is called

and: Results are limited to specified page size
scenarios.given: Admin user is authenticated and many quotas exist
scenarios.name: List quotas with custom pagination
scenarios.then: 200 OK with paginated results
scenarios.when: GET /api/admin/quota?page=2&per_page=25 is called

data.purpose: Get summary of quota information including next reset dates and current month costs across all providers.

and: Summary includes next reset dates from quota.NextProviderResets
Summary includes current month costs from usage.CostThisMonth
Summary includes provider metadata from quota.ProviderMeta
scenarios.given: Admin user is authenticated and quota data exists
scenarios.name: Get quota summary
scenarios.then: 200 OK with summary containing resets and spent amounts
scenarios.when: GET /api/admin/quota/summary is called

and: earliestReset function parses reset dates with time.Parse
Earliest reset date is determined using t.Before comparison
scenarios.given: Multiple providers with different reset schedules
scenarios.name: Summary calculates earliest reset correctly
scenarios.then: 200 OK with correct earliest reset date in summary
scenarios.when: GET /api/admin/quota/summary is called with multiple providers

data.purpose: Refresh quota data for a specific connection by ID.

and: Quota data is refreshed from external provider
scenarios.given: Admin user is authenticated and connection exists
scenarios.name: Refresh quota for valid connection
scenarios.then: 200 OK with refreshed quota data
scenarios.when: POST /api/admin/quota/conn-123/refresh is called

and: Error is returned if connection not found
scenarios.given: Admin user is authenticated but connection does not exist
scenarios.name: Refresh quota for invalid connection
scenarios.then: 404 Not Found or error response
scenarios.when: POST /api/admin/quota/invalid-id/refresh is called

data.purpose: Returns the current TLS/HTTPS configuration including validity flag, certificate cache directory, and whether the HTTPS server is currently active.

and: Response body contains data object with enabled, domain, email, acceptTOS, staging, certCache, valid, certDir, and active fields.
scenarios.given: Admin user is authenticated and TLS configuration exists in data directory.
scenarios.name: Retrieve current TLS configuration
scenarios.then: 200 OK with TLS configuration details in response body.
scenarios.when: GET /api/admin/tls is called.

and: Response body contains error message.
scenarios.given: Admin user is authenticated but TLS configuration file is corrupted or unreadable.
scenarios.name: Failed to load TLS configuration
scenarios.then: 500 Internal Server Error.
scenarios.when: GET /api/admin/tls is called.

data.purpose: Validates and persists a new TLS/HTTPS configuration. If enabled, logs that a restart is required to activate HTTPS on port 443.

and: If enabled, logs message indicating restart required to activate on :443.
If certCache is empty in request, defaults to 'certs'.
scenarios.given: Admin user is authenticated and provides valid TLS configuration payload.
scenarios.name: Update TLS configuration successfully
scenarios.then: 200 OK with ok: true in response body.
scenarios.when: PUT /api/admin/tls is called with valid JSON body.

and: Response body contains validation error message.
scenarios.given: Admin user is authenticated but provides invalid TLS configuration (e.g., missing domain when enabled).
scenarios.name: Validation failure on TLS config
scenarios.then: 400 Bad Request.
scenarios.when: PUT /api/admin/tls is called with invalid configuration.

and: Response body contains error message from JSON binding.
scenarios.given: Admin user is authenticated but sends malformed JSON or wrong types.
scenarios.name: Malformed request body
scenarios.then: 400 Bad Request.
scenarios.when: PUT /api/admin/tls is called with malformed JSON.

and: Response body contains error message from file system operation.
scenarios.given: Admin user is authenticated but server fails to write configuration to disk.
scenarios.name: Persist failure
scenarios.then: 500 Internal Server Error.
scenarios.when: PUT /api/admin/tls is called and SaveHTTPSConfig fails.

data.purpose: Returns the host's public IPv4 address as discovered via external service.

and: Response body contains data object with ip field.
scenarios.given: Admin user is authenticated and external IP discovery service is reachable.
scenarios.name: Retrieve public IP successfully
scenarios.then: 200 OK with public IP address in response body.
scenarios.when: GET /api/admin/tls/public-ip is called.

and: Response body contains error message from network operation.
scenarios.given: Admin user is authenticated but external IP discovery service is unreachable.
scenarios.name: Public IP discovery failure
scenarios.then: 502 Bad Gateway.
scenarios.when: GET /api/admin/tls/public-ip is called.

data.purpose: Resolves the requested domain and reports whether it points to the host's public IP address.

and: Response body contains data object with domain, publicIP, resolvedIP, and matches fields.
matches is true when resolvedIP equals publicIP and resolvedIP is not empty.
scenarios.given: Admin user is authenticated, provides domain query parameter, and DNS resolution succeeds.
scenarios.name: Check DNS resolution matches public IP
scenarios.then: 200 OK with DNS check results in response body.
scenarios.when: GET /api/admin/tls/check-dns?domain=example.com is called.

and: Response body contains error message indicating domain parameter is required.
scenarios.given: Admin user is authenticated but omits domain query parameter.
scenarios.name: Missing domain parameter
scenarios.then: 400 Bad Request.
scenarios.when: GET /api/admin/tls/check-dns is called without domain query parameter.

and: Response body contains error message from network operation.
resolvedIP will be empty string in successful response if DNS lookup fails.
scenarios.given: Admin user is authenticated but external IP discovery service is unreachable.
scenarios.name: Public IP discovery failure during DNS check
scenarios.then: 502 Bad Gateway.
scenarios.when: GET /api/admin/tls/check-dns?domain=example.com is called.

data.purpose: Download a backup of the database as an NDJSON stream.

and: Response headers include Content-Type: application/x-ndjson
Response headers include Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="axonrouter-backup.ndjson"
Response body is a chunked NDJSON stream of backup data
scenarios.given: Authenticated admin user
scenarios.name: Download backup
scenarios.then: 200 OK with NDJSON stream of backup data
scenarios.when: POST /api/admin/backup/download is called

data.purpose: Restore the database from a backup file upload.

and: If restore is successful, the onRestoreRestart callback is invoked after a 2-second delay
scenarios.given: Authenticated admin user with a valid backup file
scenarios.name: Restore from backup
scenarios.then: 200 OK with restore result data
scenarios.when: POST /api/admin/backup/restore is called with multipart form data containing backup file and optional password

and: Error response contains specific error message
scenarios.given: Authenticated admin user without a backup file
scenarios.name: Restore missing file
scenarios.then: 400 Bad Request with error 'backup file is required'
scenarios.when: POST /api/admin/backup/restore is called without backup file

and: Error response contains specific error message
scenarios.given: Authenticated admin user with an invalid backup file
scenarios.name: Restore invalid file
scenarios.then: 400 Bad Request with error details
scenarios.when: POST /api/admin/backup/restore is called with corrupted or invalid backup file

data.purpose: Returns paginated request logs with optional filters for provider, connection, model, combo, modality, status, date range, and search.

and: page defaults to 1
per_page defaults to 100
scenarios.given: Authenticated admin user
scenarios.name: List logs with default pagination
scenarios.then: 200 OK with paginated list of request logs
scenarios.when: GET /api/admin/logs is called without query parameters

and: Filters applied: provider_id, connection_id, model_id, combo_id, modality, status, search, start_date/end_date or since/until
scenarios.given: Authenticated admin user
scenarios.name: List logs with filters
scenarios.then: 200 OK with filtered paginated list of request logs
scenarios.when: GET /api/admin/logs?provider_id=xyz&start_date=2024-01-01 is called

data.purpose: Deletes request logs older than a specified retention window (7, 30, or 90 days).

and: older_than_days must be 7, 30, or 90
scenarios.given: Authenticated admin user
scenarios.name: Clear old logs successfully
scenarios.then: 200 OK with count of deleted rows
scenarios.when: DELETE /api/admin/logs with body {"older_than_days": 30}

and: Validation error returned
scenarios.given: Authenticated admin user
scenarios.name: Clear logs with invalid retention days
scenarios.then: 400 Bad Request with error message
scenarios.when: DELETE /api/admin/logs with body {"older_than_days": 15}

data.purpose: Returns currently in-flight proxied requests for the live in-flight panel.

and: Data sourced from active request tracking system
scenarios.given: Authenticated admin user
scenarios.name: Get active requests
scenarios.then: 200 OK with list of active/in-flight requests
scenarios.when: GET /api/admin/logs/active is called

data.purpose: Returns a single request log entry by ID with full details including tokens, latency, cost, and metadata.

and: Includes all token types, latency, status code, error message, cost, client IP, user agent
scenarios.given: Authenticated admin user
scenarios.name: Get existing log
scenarios.then: 200 OK with log details
scenarios.when: GET /api/admin/logs/123 is called

and: Log ID does not exist in database
scenarios.given: Authenticated admin user
scenarios.name: Get non-existent log
scenarios.then: 404 Not Found with error message
scenarios.when: GET /api/admin/logs/999 is called

data.purpose: Returns aggregated log statistics including provider usage, model usage, and daily usage trends.

and: hours defaults to 24
daily usage always returns 30 days

scenarios.name: Get stats with default hours
scenarios.then: 200 OK with provider_usage, model_usage, and daily_usage
scenarios.when: GET /api/admin/logs/stats is called

and: Custom time window for provider and model usage

scenarios.name: Get stats with custom hours
scenarios.then: 200 OK with aggregated stats for specified hours
scenarios.when: GET /api/admin/logs/stats?hours=48 is called

data.purpose: List returns all combos.

and: Response contains array of all combo entities
scenarios.given: Admin user is authenticated
scenarios.name: List all combos
scenarios.then: 200 OK with list of combos
scenarios.when: GET /api/admin/combos is called

data.purpose: Get returns a single combo with its steps.

and: Response includes combo details and associated steps
scenarios.given: Admin user is authenticated and combo exists
scenarios.name: Get combo by ID
scenarios.then: 200 OK with combo and steps
scenarios.when: GET /api/admin/combos/:id is called with valid ID

data.purpose: Create creates a new combo with steps.

and: Combo is persisted with associated steps
Validation checks strategy and fusion config
scenarios.given: Admin user is authenticated with valid combo data
scenarios.name: Create new combo
scenarios.then: 201 Created with new combo
scenarios.when: POST /api/admin/combos is called

data.purpose: Update modifies a combo.

and: Validates strategy via isValidComboStrategy
Parses and validates fusion config
Normalizes strategy to lowercase trimmed
scenarios.given: Admin user is authenticated with valid update data
scenarios.name: Update existing combo
scenarios.then: 200 OK with updated combo
scenarios.when: PUT /api/admin/combos/:id is called

data.purpose: Delete removes a combo.

and: Associated steps may be cascade deleted
scenarios.given: Admin user is authenticated and combo exists
scenarios.name: Delete combo
scenarios.then: 204 No Content
scenarios.when: DELETE /api/admin/combos/:id is called

data.purpose: AddStep adds a step to a combo.

and: Step is appended to combo's step sequence
scenarios.given: Admin user is authenticated with valid step data
scenarios.name: Add step to combo
scenarios.then: 201 Created with new step
scenarios.when: POST /api/admin/combos/:id/steps is called

data.purpose: RemoveStep removes a step from a combo.

and: Step is removed from combo's step sequence
scenarios.given: Admin user is authenticated and step exists
scenarios.name: Remove step from combo
scenarios.then: 204 No Content
scenarios.when: DELETE /api/admin/combos/:id/steps/:stepId is called

data.purpose: Query param `window` is the lookback window in seconds (default 86400, max 2592000).

and: Default window is 86400 seconds (1 day)
Maximum allowed window is 2592000 seconds (30 days)
Metrics aggregated from request logs
scenarios.given: Admin user is authenticated
scenarios.name: Get combo metrics
scenarios.then: 200 OK with aggregated metrics
scenarios.when: GET /api/admin/combos/metrics is called

data.purpose: SeedDefaults seeds default combos.

and: Default combos are created if they don't exist
Idempotent operation
scenarios.given: Admin user is authenticated
scenarios.name: Seed default combos
scenarios.then: 200 OK with seeding results
scenarios.when: POST /api/admin/combos/seed-defaults is called

data.purpose: Returns a simple liveness check for load balancers and dashboards. Reports ok/degraded/error based on in-memory store and usage-tracker health, never blocks on DB access. Includes version info and password-change flag.

and: Response body contains status, db, version, latest_version, update_available, must_change_password fields
scenarios.given: Application is running with valid store and tracker
scenarios.name: Healthy system
scenarios.then: 200 OK with status 'ok'
scenarios.when: GET /health is called without authentication

and: Response body contains status 'degraded' and db 'degraded'
scenarios.given: Usage tracker has dropped events indicating write pipeline saturation
scenarios.name: Degraded system
scenarios.then: 200 OK with status 'degraded'
scenarios.when: GET /health is called without authentication

and: Response body contains status 'error' and db 'error'
scenarios.given: Store or tracker is nil
scenarios.name: Unhealthy system
scenarios.then: 503 Service Unavailable with status 'error'
scenarios.when: GET /health is called without authentication

and: must_change_password is true when admin_password_changed flag is missing but password hash exists
must_change_password is false when admin_password_changed is 'true'
scenarios.given: Database has admin password settings
scenarios.name: Password change required flag
scenarios.then: Response includes must_change_password boolean based on settings flags
scenarios.when: GET /health is called

data.purpose: Forces a fresh fetch of the latest GitHub release and returns current version, latest version, and whether an update is available.

and: Response contains version, latest_version, update_available fields
scenarios.given: Version checker is initialized and GitHub API is reachable
scenarios.name: Successful update check
scenarios.then: 200 OK with version comparison info
scenarios.when: GET /admin/check-update is called by admin

and: Response contains error message
scenarios.given: Version checker is nil
scenarios.name: Checker unavailable
scenarios.then: 503 Service Unavailable
scenarios.when: GET /admin/check-update is called

and: Response contains error message from GitHub API
scenarios.given: Version checker is initialized but GitHub API call fails
scenarios.name: GitHub API failure
scenarios.then: 502 Bad Gateway with error details
scenarios.when: GET /admin/check-update is called by admin

data.purpose: Returns the project's CHANGELOG markdown via the version checker. Used by dashboard to avoid CORS and ad-blocker issues from fetching raw.githubusercontent.com directly.

and: Response contains markdown field with CHANGELOG content
scenarios.given: Version checker is initialized and changelog is available
scenarios.name: Successful changelog retrieval
scenarios.then: 200 OK with markdown string
scenarios.when: GET /admin/changelog is called by admin

and: Response contains error message
scenarios.given: Version checker is nil
scenarios.name: Checker unavailable
scenarios.then: 503 Service Unavailable
scenarios.when: GET /admin/changelog is called

and: Response contains error message from changelog fetch
scenarios.given: Version checker is initialized but changelog fetch fails
scenarios.name: Changelog fetch failure
scenarios.then: 502 Bad Gateway with error details
scenarios.when: GET /admin/changelog is called by admin

data.purpose: Returns operational counters for observability including connection health, usage buffer state, rate-limited/quota-exhausted connection counts, and today's aggregated usage statistics.

and: Response contains buffer_length, healthy_connections, dropped_usage_events, rate_limited, quota_exhausted, requests_today, tokens_today, cost_today
scenarios.given: Database is accessible and store/tracker are initialized
scenarios.name: Successful metrics retrieval
scenarios.then: 200 OK with operational counters
scenarios.when: GET /admin/metrics is called by admin

and: rate_limited and quota_exhausted are counts of active connections with those statuses
requests_today, tokens_today, cost_today are aggregated from usage data
scenarios.given: Database has connection and usage data
scenarios.name: Metrics with active connections
scenarios.then: 200 OK with accurate counts and aggregates
scenarios.when: GET /admin/metrics is called by admin

data.purpose: Returns the key name and masked device list for the given API key.

scenarios.name: Retrieve devices for existing API key
scenarios.then: 200 OK with key metadata and device list

scenarios.given: Admin is authenticated and API key with ID does not exist

scenarios.when: GET /admin/devices/:id is called with invalid key ID

and: Response body contains error message
scenarios.given: Database error occurs during query
scenarios.name: Database error
scenarios.then: 500 Internal Server Error
scenarios.when: GET /admin/devices/:id is called

data.purpose: Returns aggregated dashboard statistics including today summary and system metrics.

scenarios.name: Retrieve dashboard stats

data.purpose: Returns provider summary including disabled reasons so the dashboard can show why accounts are disabled.

scenarios.name: Retrieve provider summary
scenarios.then: 200 OK with JSON array of provider summaries including disabled reasons

data.purpose: Returns the last N log entries for the dashboard.

Logs are ordered by timestamp descending

scenarios.then: 200 OK with JSON array of last N log entries
scenarios.when: GET /api/admin/dashboard/recent-logs is called with optional ?limit=N parameter

data.purpose: Downloads the latest release binary, verifies its SHA256 checksum, and replaces the current binary with the new version.

and: Response contains restart instructions with command and hint
New binary is written to temporary path and atomically swapped
scenarios.given: Admin user is authenticated and version checker has latest release info
scenarios.name: Successful upgrade
scenarios.then: 200 OK with restart instructions in response body
scenarios.when: POST /api/admin/upgrade is called

and: Error message indicates checksum verification failed
scenarios.given: Admin user is authenticated but downloaded binary has invalid checksum
scenarios.name: Upgrade with invalid checksum
scenarios.then: 500 Internal Server Error
scenarios.when: POST /api/admin/upgrade is called

and: Error message indicates download failure
scenarios.given: Admin user is authenticated but release download fails
scenarios.name: Upgrade download failure
scenarios.then: 500 Internal Server Error
scenarios.when: POST /api/admin/upgrade is called

data.purpose: Restarts the systemd service to apply a binary upgrade by delegating to a system-scoped unit.

and: Service restart is triggered via system-scoped systemd unit
scenarios.given: Admin user is authenticated and binary has been upgraded
scenarios.name: Successful restart
scenarios.then: 200 OK with restart initiated

and: Error message indicates systemd command failure
scenarios.given: Admin user is authenticated but systemd restart command fails
scenarios.name: Restart failure
scenarios.then: 500 Internal Server Error
scenarios.when: POST /api/admin/restart is called

data.purpose: Read the log file from the end and return up to maxConsoleLines lines of the console log for admin monitoring.

scenarios.name: Retrieve console logs successfully

data.purpose: Create a new order from the current cart.

scenarios.given: Authenticated user with valid cart

scenarios.then: 201 Created with orderId in response body

data.purpose: Change the admin password. Restricted from master API key authentication to prevent unauthorized password changes if the master key is leaked.

and: Response contains error code 'master_action_forbidden'
HTTP status is 403 Forbidden
scenarios.given: Request is authenticated with master API key
scenarios.name: Master key blocked from password change
scenarios.then: Request is rejected with 403 Forbidden
scenarios.when: POST /admin/api/v1/change-password is called with master key auth

and: Request proceeds to handler
scenarios.given: Request is authenticated with session-based dashboard auth
scenarios.name: Session auth allowed for password change
scenarios.then: Request is allowed to proceed
scenarios.when: POST /admin/api/v1/change-password is called with session auth

data.purpose: Defer a required password change. Restricted from master API key authentication to prevent bypassing security policies if the master key is leaked.

and: Response contains error code 'master_action_forbidden'
HTTP status is 403 Forbidden
scenarios.given: Request is authenticated with master API key
scenarios.name: Master key blocked from deferring password change
scenarios.then: Request is rejected with 403 Forbidden
scenarios.when: POST /admin/api/v1/defer-password-change is called with master key auth

and: Request proceeds to handler
scenarios.given: Request is authenticated with session-based dashboard auth
scenarios.name: Session auth allowed to defer password change
scenarios.then: Request is allowed to proceed
scenarios.when: POST /admin/api/v1/defer-password-change is called with session auth

data.purpose: Retrieve the master API key. Restricted from master API key authentication to prevent key exposure if the master key is leaked.

and: Response contains error code 'master_action_forbidden'
HTTP status is 403 Forbidden
scenarios.given: Request is authenticated with master API key
scenarios.name: Master key blocked from retrieving master key
scenarios.then: Request is rejected with 403 Forbidden
scenarios.when: GET /admin/api/v1/developers/master-key is called with master key auth

and: Request proceeds to handler
scenarios.given: Request is authenticated with session-based dashboard auth
scenarios.name: Session auth allowed to retrieve master key
scenarios.then: Request is allowed to proceed
scenarios.when: GET /admin/api/v1/developers/master-key is called with session auth

data.purpose: Regenerate the master API key. Restricted from master API key authentication to prevent unauthorized key rotation if the master key is leaked.

and: Response contains error code 'master_action_forbidden'
HTTP status is 403 Forbidden
scenarios.given: Request is authenticated with master API key
scenarios.name: Master key blocked from regenerating master key
scenarios.then: Request is rejected with 403 Forbidden
scenarios.when: POST /admin/api/v1/developers/master-key/regenerate is called with master key auth

and: Request proceeds to handler
scenarios.given: Request is authenticated with session-based dashboard auth
scenarios.name: Session auth allowed to regenerate master key
scenarios.then: Request is allowed to proceed
scenarios.when: POST /admin/api/v1/developers/master-key/regenerate is called with session auth

data.purpose: Trigger a system upgrade. Restricted from master API key authentication to prevent unauthorized system modifications if the master key is leaked.

and: Response contains error code 'master_action_forbidden'
HTTP status is 403 Forbidden
scenarios.given: Request is authenticated with master API key
scenarios.name: Master key blocked from system upgrade
scenarios.then: Request is rejected with 403 Forbidden
scenarios.when: POST /admin/api/v1/upgrade is called with master key auth

and: Request proceeds to handler
scenarios.given: Request is authenticated with session-based dashboard auth
scenarios.name: Session auth allowed for system upgrade
scenarios.then: Request is allowed to proceed
scenarios.when: POST /admin/api/v1/upgrade is called with session auth

data.purpose: Restart the system. Restricted from master API key authentication to prevent unauthorized system restart if the master key is leaked.

and: Response contains error code 'master_action_forbidden'
HTTP status is 403 Forbidden
scenarios.given: Request is authenticated with master API key
scenarios.name: Master key blocked from system restart
scenarios.then: Request is rejected with 403 Forbidden
scenarios.when: POST /admin/api/v1/restart is called with master key auth

and: Request proceeds to handler
scenarios.given: Request is authenticated with session-based dashboard auth
scenarios.name: Session auth allowed for system restart
scenarios.then: Request is allowed to proceed
scenarios.when: POST /admin/api/v1/restart is called with session auth

data.purpose: Download a system backup. Restricted from master API key authentication to prevent data exfiltration if the master key is leaked.

and: Response contains error code 'master_action_forbidden'
HTTP status is 403 Forbidden
scenarios.given: Request is authenticated with master API key
scenarios.name: Master key blocked from backup download
scenarios.then: Request is rejected with 403 Forbidden
scenarios.when: POST /admin/api/v1/backup/download is called with master key auth

and: Request proceeds to handler
scenarios.given: Request is authenticated with session-based dashboard auth
scenarios.name: Session auth allowed for backup download
scenarios.then: Request is allowed to proceed
scenarios.when: POST /admin/api/v1/backup/download is called with session auth

data.purpose: Restore a system backup. Restricted from master API key authentication to prevent unauthorized data restoration if the master key is leaked.

and: Response contains error code 'master_action_forbidden'
HTTP status is 403 Forbidden
scenarios.given: Request is authenticated with master API key
scenarios.name: Master key blocked from backup restore
scenarios.then: Request is rejected with 403 Forbidden
scenarios.when: POST /admin/api/v1/backup/restore is called with master key auth

and: Request proceeds to handler
scenarios.given: Request is authenticated with session-based dashboard auth
scenarios.name: Session auth allowed for backup restore
scenarios.then: Request is allowed to proceed
scenarios.when: POST /admin/api/v1/backup/restore is called with session auth

data.purpose: Update TLS configuration. Restricted from master API key authentication to prevent unauthorized security configuration changes if the master key is leaked.

and: Response contains error code 'master_action_forbidden'
HTTP status is 403 Forbidden
scenarios.given: Request is authenticated with master API key
scenarios.name: Master key blocked from TLS config update
scenarios.then: Request is rejected with 403 Forbidden
scenarios.when: PUT /admin/api/v1/tls-config is called with master key auth

and: Request proceeds to handler
scenarios.given: Request is authenticated with session-based dashboard auth
scenarios.name: Session auth allowed for TLS config update
scenarios.then: Request is allowed to proceed
scenarios.when: PUT /admin/api/v1/tls-config is called with session auth
